$SHOP says that starting this week, millions of merchants can sell directly to ChatGPT users through agentic storefronts.

The rollout opens a new distribution channel that brings Shopify merchants natively into conversational commerce.

Omnicom (one of world’s largest ad companies) has launched an audit into $TTD fees suggesting this is no longer just a Publicis dispute but a broader industry reckoning over fee transparency.

For a company built on being the clean alternative to the walled gardens… The Trade Desk is starting to look like it has a very real identity crisis.

$MSFT announced an AI collaboration for nuclear energy with $NVDA.

The partnership points to growing interest in applying AI to nuclear operations and infrastructure.

$CELH down over 15% in the past week after $COST rolled out Kirkland Signature energy drinks at just $0.70 per can with 200 mg of caffeine.

That is a major price undercut versus most energy drinks closer to $2 per can & if Costco gets the taste right it could take real share.

$AMZN Zoox plans to launch its robotaxi service in Austin & Miami later this year. 

The company says it has already served 350K riders, has about 500K people on its waitlist and has been testing vehicles in both cities for nearly two years.

$CRCL falls ~15% after the CLARITY Act deal signals no yield on stablecoin balances by allowing only activity-based rewards.

That weakens a key part of the bull case by making USDC harder to evolve from a payments utility into a real store-of-value product.

$UBER is reportedly close to acquiring Blacklane for a high triple-digit million dollar amount.

The deal would expand Uber’s reach further into premium ride services.

MACRO DATA JUST DROPPED

• Manufacturing PMI 52.4 vs. Est. 51.3
• Services PMI 51.1 vs. Est. 51.5
• Composite PMI: 51.4

This should ease the recession fears that started creeping into the market last week and suggests the bear case may have gotten ahead of itself.

$GRAB said it plans to repurchase up to $400M of shares over the next four months.

That would represent 80% of its total $500M buyback program.

Mizuho is defending $SOFI against the Muddy Waters short report:

• The $312M JPM transaction looks like a true sale (not hidden debt)
• The 3.9% discount rate looks reasonable for a ~4-year loan duration
• SOFI’s filings clearly distinguish true sales from secured borrowings
• Fitch data & company disclosures align more closely with management’s figures
• The charge-off rate appears closer to 4.4% (not 6.1% alleged in the short report)

$CRWV pushed back on the GPU depreciation debate saying the idea that AI chips become obsolete in 18 months doesn't match how customers actually buy compute.

He said most contracts run about five years & older GPUs still retain value as new use cases emerge.

"We're focused on and worried about the rise in oil prices, but it's the rise in interest rates that can actually have a broader negative impact on the global economy..."

The Sweet 16 will be all power-conference schools: Big Ten, SEC, ACC, Big 12, and Big East.

Since the field expanded to 64 in 1985, it has only happened twice: last year and this year. This year, we can now say it with three games left to finish, as they feature power conference schools playing each other.

NIL + the transfer portal are hollowing out March Madness.

The old Cinderella model is dying because mid-majors can’t keep the veteran cores that used to make these runs. Once those players establish themselves, they jump to bigger stages and bigger money at power-conference schools.

The only double-digit seed left is No. 11 Texas. One of the richest athletic departments in college sports is not what comes to mind when you think “Cinderella.”

A bracket with Siena, Miami (Ohio), Saint Louis, and/or High Point still alive would have felt a lot more like March Madness.

“We expect both DRAM and NAND industry bit demand in calendar 2026 to be constrained by supply. We continue to expect supply-demand conditions for both DRAM and NAND to remain tight beyond calendar 2026.”

📺 $PLTR & MEMORY STOCKS: THE KEY LEADERSHIP CANDIDATES

While $SPY and $QQQ are trading below the 200-day, names like $MU, $SNDK, and $WDC are holding above key moving averages, signaling relative strength. 

The recent bounce was driven by oversold names and short covering, not real leadership, which is why stronger stocks didn’t lead. 

There’s no confirmed shift yet—just consolidation and lack of urgency to chase highs. If the market stabilizes, these leaders are best positioned to expand. 

$PLTR also looks interesting. It broke out of a sideways consolidation, which is typically a constructive pattern. It also held up even as $SPY and $QQQ were pushing toward lows, which is exactly what you want to see in a potential leader.

If the market stabilizes or turns higher, $PLTR has the setup to continue outperforming.

So, focus on relative strength, not noise, and wait for alignment between the market and true leaders before getting aggressive.

Overnight, Iran launched missile and drone attacks on Eilat, Dimona, Tel Aviv, and U.S. bases in the region.

Saudi Arabia said it intercepted a drone in its eastern region, Kuwait reported power line disruptions after an Iranian attack, and sirens sounded in Bahrain.

Fars also reported U.S.-Israeli strikes damaged a gas pressure-regulation plant and an administrative building in Isfahan, along with a pipeline supplying the Khorramshahr Combined Cycle Power Plant.

FinanceAsia recently explored how AI is transforming Hong Kong’s financial sector, with insights from @Dr_XiaolinChen, Head of International at KraneShares, on the opportunities and risks of rapid adoption.

The article highlights AI’s growing role in areas like IPO processes, investment decisions, and compliance. 

Xiaolin underscores the continued importance of human judgment in maintaining trust, while also pointing to AI-driven asset management as an emerging “third way” between active and passive investing.

@FinanceAsia

🚩The 60/40 portfolio has one job. When stocks fall, bonds hold. In five of six major stress episodes since late 2022, it has not done that job.

Now for the part that makes this worse.🧵

Japan holds $1.225 trillion in US Treasuries. And right now, three separate forces are pushing them toward the exit at the same time. 

The first one starts with oil.

Everyone obsesses over $SPY. Watch the plumbing: $HYG vs $TLT. When credit stops playing nice, equities get religion.
$VIX around the low-20s is the most dangerous regime. Not panic. Not complacency. It’s where people keep buying every dip… until they run out of dips to buy.
